dom: King of the gods, god of sky and thunder (Roman: Jupiter)
fa: Overthrew his father Cronus to become ruler of Mount Olympus

dom: Queen of the gods, goddess of marriage (Roman: Juno)
fa: Zeus's wife and sister, often depicted punishing his many lovers and children

dom: God of the sea, earthquakes and horses (Roman: Neptune)
fa: Could cause earthquakes by striking the ground with his trident

dom: God of the underworld and the dead (Roman: Pluto)
fa: Ruled the underworld but rarely left it, making him one of the least depicted major gods

dom: Goddess of the harvest and agriculture (Roman: Ceres)
fa: Her grief over her daughter Persephone's abduction was said to cause winter

dom: Goddess of wisdom and strategic warfare (Roman: Minerva)
fa: Born fully grown and armored from Zeus's forehead

dom: God of music, prophecy, archery and the sun
fa: One of the few Olympians to keep the same name in both Greek and Roman mythology

dom: Goddess of the hunt and the moon (Roman: Diana)
fa: Twin sister of Apollo, sworn to eternal virginity and protector of wild animals

dom: God of war (Roman: Mars)
fa: Unlike the more strategic Athena, Ares embodied the brutal, chaotic aspects of war

dom: Goddess of love and beauty (Roman: Venus)
fa: Said to have been born from sea foam near the island of Cyprus

dom: God of fire and the forge (Roman: Vulcan)
fa: The blacksmith of the gods, said to have crafted Zeus's thunderbolts

dom: Messenger god, god of trade and travelers (Roman: Mercury)
fa: Known for his speed, he guided souls to the underworld as well as delivering messages

dom: God of wine, festivity and theater (Roman: Bacchus)
fa: The only Olympian said to have had a mortal mother

dom: Goddess of the hearth and home (Roman: Vesta)
fa: Gave up her seat among the twelve Olympians to Dionysus, preferring a quiet domestic role

dom: Queen of the underworld, goddess of spring (Roman: Proserpina)
fa: Her yearly return from the underworld was said to bring about spring

dom: God of love and desire (Roman: Cupid)
fa: His arrows were said to make anyone struck by them fall instantly in love

dom: Goddess of victory
fa: Often shown as a winged figure crowning victors, later inspiring the sportswear brand's name

dom: Goddess of the rainbow and messenger of the gods
fa: Served as a messenger for Hera in particular, traveling along rainbows

dom: God of sleep (Roman: Somnus)
sym: Poppy flower, horn of sleep-inducing liquid
fa: Twin brother of Thanatos, the god of death

dom: God of death
fa: Personified peaceful death, unlike the violent death goddesses called the Keres

dom: Goddess of retribution and revenge
fa: Punished those who succumbed to hubris, or excessive pride against the gods

dom: Goddess of fortune and chance (Roman: Fortuna)
fa: Her favor was believed to be entirely unpredictable and could shift a city's fate overnight

dom: God of the wild, shepherds and rustic music (Roman: Faunus)
sym: Pan flute, goat legs and horns
fa: His sudden appearance was said to cause sudden, irrational fear, giving us the word 'panic'

dom: Goddess of magic, crossroads and witchcraft
fa: Associated with the night and often depicted with three faces looking in different directions

dom: Primordial goddess and personification of the Earth
fa: Considered the mother of the Titans and, through them, the ancestor of nearly all Greek gods

dom: Primordial god and personification of the sky
fa: Was overthrown and castrated by his son Cronus in one of Greek mythology's most violent myths

dom: King of the Titans, god of time and the harvest (Roman: Saturn)
fa: Swallowed his own children to prevent a prophecy that one would overthrow him, but was outwitted by Zeus

dom: Titan goddess of fertility, mother of the Olympians
fa: Tricked Cronus by giving him a stone wrapped in cloth to swallow instead of the infant Zeus

dom: Titan associated with fire and forethought
fa: Stole fire from the gods to give to humanity and was punished with eternal torment as a result

dom: Titan condemned to hold up the sky
fa: Punished after the Titans' war against the Olympians to bear the heavens on his shoulders forever

dom: Titan god of the sun (Roman: Sol)
fa: Believed to drive a golden chariot pulling the sun across the sky each day

dom: Titan goddess of the moon (Roman: Luna)
fa: Fell in love with the mortal Endymion, who was granted eternal sleep so she could visit him forever

dom: Titan goddess of the dawn (Roman: Aurora)
fa: Said to open the gates of heaven each morning to let Helios's sun chariot through

dom: God of medicine and healing
sym: Rod with a serpent coiled around it
fa: His symbol, the Rod of Asclepius, is still used today as a medical emblem

dom: Messenger god of the sea, son of Poseidon
fa: Could calm or stir up the seas by blowing into his conch shell

dom: Primordial goddess and personification of night
sym: Dark veil, chariot of black horses
fa: One of the very first beings to exist according to Greek cosmogony, even feared by Zeus

dom: Primordial void from which all creation began
fa: Not a god but a formless state of nothingness that existed before the universe took shape

dom: Hero famed for superhuman strength (Roman: Hercules)
fa: Completed twelve immense labors as penance, including slaying the Nemean Lion

dom: Hero of the Trojan War
fa: Was said to be invulnerable except for his heel, giving us the phrase 'Achilles' heel'

dom: Hero and king of Ithaca (Roman: Ulysses)
fa: His ten-year journey home from the Trojan War inspired Homer's epic 'The Odyssey'

dom: Hero who slew the gorgon Medusa
fa: Used a reflective shield to safely behead Medusa without meeting her petrifying gaze

dom: Hero and legendary king of Athens
fa: Defeated the Minotaur in the labyrinth of Crete with the help of a thread given by Ariadne

dom: Hero who led the quest for the Golden Fleece
fa: Led a crew of heroes known as the Argonauts on his famous quest

dom: Legendary musician and poet
fa: Journeyed into the underworld to retrieve his wife Eurydice, but lost her by looking back too soon

dom: Tragic king of Thebes
fa: Unknowingly fulfilled a prophecy of killing his father and marrying his mother

dom: Trojan hero, legendary ancestor of the Romans
fa: His journey from fallen Troy to found the Roman people is told in Virgil's epic 'The Aeneid'

dom: Hero who tamed the winged horse Pegasus
fa: Used Pegasus to defeat the monstrous Chimera

dom: Heroine known for her speed as a huntress
fa: Vowed only to marry a man who could outrun her in a footrace

dom: Figure known for his flight and fall
fa: Ignored his father Daedalus's warning and flew too close to the sun, melting his wings

dom: Legendary inventor and craftsman
fa: Built the labyrinth of Crete that housed the Minotaur, and later designed wings to escape it

dom: Figure condemned to eternal futile labor
fa: Punished by the gods to forever push a boulder up a hill, only for it to roll back down each time

dom: Figure condemned to eternal, unreachable temptation
fa: Punished with food and water forever just out of reach, giving us the word 'tantalize'

dom: The first mortal woman in Greek mythology
fa: Opened a forbidden jar releasing all evils into the world, leaving only hope trapped inside

dom: Figure known for his beauty and self-obsession
fa: Fell in love with his own reflection and wasted away, giving us the word 'narcissism'

dom: Mountain nymph cursed to only repeat others' words
fa: Cursed by Hera and later faded away from unrequited love for Narcissus, leaving only her voice

dom: Legendary sculptor
fa: Fell in love with a statue he carved, which the goddess Aphrodite brought to life

dom: Legendary king granted a golden touch
fa: Granted a wish that turned everything he touched to gold, which nearly caused him to starve

dom: Mortal weaver transformed into the first spider
fa: Challenged Athena to a weaving contest and was transformed into a spider as punishment for her pride

dom: Gorgon whose gaze turned onlookers to stone
fa: Was once a mortal woman before being transformed into a monster by Athena

dom: Legendary figure whose abduction sparked the Trojan War
fa: Called 'the face that launched a thousand ships' due to the war fought over her

dom: Trojan princess cursed with true but disbelieved prophecies
fa: Cursed by Apollo so that no one would ever believe her accurate predictions

dom: Princess who helped Theseus escape the labyrinth
fa: Gave Theseus a ball of thread to navigate the labyrinth and later became Dionysus's wife

dom: Sorceress skilled in potions and transformation
fa: Turned Odysseus's crew into pigs in Homer's 'The Odyssey'

dom: Wife of Odysseus, known for her loyalty
fa: Delayed remarriage for years by secretly unweaving a burial shroud she claimed to be finishing

dom: Princess rescued by the hero Perseus
fa: Was chained to a rock as a sacrifice to a sea monster before being saved by Perseus

dom: Princess associated with the origin of the continent's name
fa: Was carried away by Zeus disguised as a bull, and the continent of Europe is said to be named after her

dom: Trojan prince taken to serve as cupbearer to the gods
fa: Was abducted by Zeus in the form of an eagle to become the gods' cupbearer on Olympus

dom: Wise centaur, teacher of many Greek heroes
fa: Unlike most centaurs, he was known for wisdom and kindness, and tutored Achilles and Jason

dom: Goddess of the sea, wife of Poseidon
fa: Ruled the sea alongside Poseidon and was often depicted riding a dolphin or sea creature

dom: God of dreams, son of Hypnos
fa: Could take on any human form in people's dreams, giving us the word 'morph'

dom: Goddess of strife and discord
fa: Threw a golden apple inscribed 'for the fairest' among the goddesses, indirectly sparking the Trojan War

dom: God of satire, mockery and complaint
fa: Was eventually expelled from Olympus for endlessly criticizing the other gods

dom: Roman god of beginnings, transitions and doorways
fa: Had no Greek equivalent and gave his name to the month of January

dom: Roman god of boundaries and landmarks
fa: Worshipped as the protector of property lines, with stone markers placed in his honor
